market_context: US equity markets are mid-session on Thursday with mixed signals: QQQ up 2.7% over 5 days showing tech strength, but VIX elevated at 17.84 (+4.5% 5-day) indicating underlying volatility concerns. Treasury yields stable at 4.48% with crypto weakness (BTC -1.6%, ETH -3.1%) potentially pressuring risk appetite into close.

predictions.reasoning: SPY showing modest 5-day gains (+1.2%) with QQQ outperforming (+2.7%), suggesting selective tech rotation. VIX elevation to 17.84 creates mild headwind, but stable Treasury yields and mid-day positioning favor modest close above resistance. Target reflects +0.39% move.

QQQ momentum strongest among indices (+2.7% 5-day). NVDA, MSFT, AAPL collectively driving tech strength. Crypto weakness (ETH -3.1%) is headwind but not sufficient to reverse intraday trend. Target +0.53% reflects continuation of established uptrend.

NVDA at $225.83 showing vulnerability as VIX rises and crypto weakness signals risk-off sentiment. Semiconductor sector often leads reversals in volatility spikes. Elevated VIX (+4.5% 5-day) suggests profit-taking into close. Target -1.18% reflects modest pullback.

TSLA at $445.27 vulnerable to broader risk-off as VIX elevation and crypto weakness suggest flight to safety. Growth stocks typically underperform in volatility regimes. DIA weakness (-0.4% 5-day) indicates defensive rotation. Target -0.78% reflects modest reversion.

Bitcoin already down 1.6% over 5 days at $79,362.80 with elevated VIX suggesting continued risk-off. Treasury yields stable at 4.48% reduce carry-trade appeal. Crypto weakness typically accelerates into US market close. Target -1.46% reflects continuation of downtrend.
